---
title: "Cost & the business case"
pagetitle: "Skein Lite: what it costs, and the proof behind it"
execute:
  freeze: true
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import json
S = json.load(open("../evaluation/reports/site_stats.json"))
C = S["cost"]
CM = json.load(open("../evaluation/reports/cost_model.json"))
B = CM["text_turn_breakdown_70b"]
ratio = f"{C['human_vs_ai_ratio']:.0f}"
turn = f"{C['text_turn_usd']:.4f}"
human_turn = f"{C['human_turn_usd']:.4f}"
session = f"{CM['per_session']['text_70b']:.4f}"
human_session = f"{CM['per_session']['human_agent']:.2f}"
voice_session = f"{CM['per_session']['voice_70b']:.2f}"
voice_vs_human = f"{CM['per_session']['human_agent']/CM['per_session']['voice_70b']:.0f}"
frontier_lo = f"{CM['per_turn']['text_sonnet5']*8:.2f}"
frontier_hi = f"{CM['per_turn']['text_opus']*8:.2f}"
frontier_vs_human_lo = f"{CM['per_session']['human_agent']/(CM['per_turn']['text_opus']*8):.0f}"
frontier_vs_human_hi = f"{CM['per_session']['human_agent']/(CM['per_turn']['text_sonnet5']*8):.0f}"
```

::: {.eyebrow}
What a conversation costs, and how the number is built
:::

A text session with Sara costs about **`{python} ratio`× less** than a human agent handling the same conversation. Every figure on this page comes from one transparent cost model and regenerates from one command:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=. uv run python -m mlops.cost_model
```

## The assumptions

Provider **list prices** (2026), each tied to the stage it pays for:

| Stage | Provider / unit | Price |
|---|---|---|
| Generation (workhorse) | Groq Llama 3.3 70B | \$0.59 / \$0.79 per 1M in/out tokens |
| Routing tie-break | Groq Llama 3.1 8B | \$0.05 / \$0.08 per 1M in/out tokens |
| Embedding | Cohere embed-v4 | \$0.12 per 1M input tokens |
| Rerank | Cohere rerank v3.5 | \$0.002 per search |
| Human agent | fully-loaded rate | \$20.00 per hour |

Usage per turn and per session:

| Assumption | Value | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Prompt tokens | `{python} CM["assumptions"]["prompt_tokens"]` | retrieved context + history + system |
| Completion tokens | `{python} CM["assumptions"]["completion_tokens"]` | a concise, grounded answer |
| Turns per session | `{python} CM["assumptions"]["session_turns"]` | a typical shopper conversation |
| 8B tie-break share | ~15% | only ambiguous turns pay for the call |
| Human handle time | 4 min / turn | the lever the whole ratio turns on |

## The math, stage by stage

One text turn is the sum of the stages it actually runs:

| Stage | Calculation | Cost |
|---|---|---:|
| Routing | (1500 × \$0.05 + 250 × \$0.08) / 1M × 15% | \$`{python} f"{B['routing']:.6f}"` |
| Retrieval | 25 tok × \$0.12/1M + \$0.002 rerank | \$`{python} f"{B['retrieval']:.6f}"` |
| Generation | 1500 × \$0.59/1M + 250 × \$0.79/1M | \$`{python} f"{B['generation']:.6f}"` |
| **Text turn total** | | **\$`{python} turn`** |

One session is eight of those turns. The human baseline is a fully-loaded agent's time:

| | Per turn | × 8 turns |
|---|---:|---:|
| Sara (text) | \$`{python} turn` | **\$`{python} session`** |
| A human agent | \$20/hr × 4 min = \$`{python} human_turn` | **\$`{python} human_session`** |

\$`{python} human_session` ÷ \$`{python} session` ≈ **`{python} ratio`×**

That is the whole derivation. Change the handle time or the model and the ratio moves, in the open, from constants you can see.

## The full comparison

| Option | Per session | Versus a human | The trade |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| **Sara (text)** | **\$`{python} session`** | **~`{python} ratio`× cheaper** | handles the routine majority, escalates the hard cases |
| Sara (voice) | \$`{python} voice_session` | ~`{python} voice_vs_human`× cheaper | the premium voice, not the model, is most of the cost |
| A frontier model in her place | \$`{python} frontier_lo` – \$`{python} frontier_hi` | ~`{python} frontier_vs_human_lo`–`{python} frontier_vs_human_hi`× cheaper | `{python} CM["ratios_vs_text_70b"]["sonnet5"]`–`{python} CM["ratios_vs_text_70b"]["opus"]`× the text cost, for no measured quality gain |
| A human agent | \$`{python} human_session` | 1× (baseline) | the gold standard for the hard cases, too costly for the routine ones |

## What it means for the business

The case is not "replace the humans". It is deflection: Sara resolves the routine majority, so the human team spends its expensive minutes on the shoppers who need them. The containment rate is measured on live traffic (`/api/admin/business`), so the saving is tracked, not assumed.

The frontier row is the same discipline in miniature: it would 3 to 5× the per-turn cost, and the [graded golden set](evaluation.qmd#the-gate-in-action-a-cheaper-model-held) showed no quality gain to pay for, so the money stays where it buys something.

## The honest caveats

- These are list prices and a bottom-up estimate, not a metered invoice. Volume discounts and busier sessions move them.
- The 4-minute handle time and the \$20/hour loaded rate are the two levers the ratio turns on. Both are constants in `mlops/cost_model.py`; substitute your own and rerun.
- These figures are the default deterministic path. The opt-in LangGraph corrective lane (`CHAT_BRAIN=graph`) costs more on the hard tail: up to three generations and a few extra retrievals per turn, still capped by the same per-turn `TurnBudget`. That higher cost, for a narrow quality gain, is exactly why it is opt-in and not the default.
- This page reads its numbers from the model's committed report, so it cannot drift from the code.
